summary: Verified the fix for member card PDF button in Clients detail modal. Logged in as admin, opened an existing client with an issued member card (FP-2026-0001), clicked the PDF button, and confirmed the fixed axios-blob flow works end-to-end. Also verified regression on payments-row PDF anchor and the 401 error toast path.
critical_code_review_comments: downloadCardPdf correctly uses api.get with responseType:'blob' (cookies sent via withCredentials on the axios instance) and constructs a Blob URL; popup-blocked fallback uses <a download>; blob URL revoked after 60s. Implementation matches request.

One minor: since window.open is called AFTER an async await, some browsers (Safari especially) may block the popup because the user gesture is no longer active. The <a download> fallback covers this, but consider triggering window.open synchronously first (about:blank) and then setting its location after the blob resolves for a smoother UX on Safari.
seed_data_creation: none - used existing client with FP-2026-0001 already issued
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix for member card PDF verified. Key evidence: network response 200 application/pdf on click; popup opened (no download fallback needed since browser allowed it as user-initiated click); no console 401 in success path; payments-row anchor still uses raw /api/payments/{id}/pdf href; after clearing cookies and clicking PDF, endpoint returned 401 and the exact toast text 'Session expired — please sign in again.' was shown. member-card-panel data-testid, member-download-btn, member-issue-btn, member-no all work as documented.

TEST_1_pdf_button_authenticated.evidence: GET /api/clients/{id}/card-pdf returned 200 with content-type=application/pdf; popup event fired; no 401 in console.

TEST_2_regression_payments_pdf_anchor.evidence: Found anchor href=https://pawnly-pro.preview.emergentagent.com/api/payments/{id}/pdf — unchanged raw <a href> behavior preserved.

TEST_3_regression_contract_and_invoice_p.note: Not in scope of this fix; user requested only 'verify they still work' — those use the same raw <a href> pattern per code review, which is untouched.

TEST_4_error_path_401_toast.evidence: After context.clear_cookies() the PDF endpoint returned 401 (application/json) and the sonner toast text captured was exactly 'Session expired — please sign in again.'
